name: Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop
name_original: La Vía Verde del Serpis – Circular desde Villalonga hasta Lorxa
description: A difficult 18.3-mile touring cycling loop with 2404 feet of elevation gain, featuring the Serpis Greenway and challenging Coll de la Safor.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vilallonga / Villalonga offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features Mediterranean coastlines, fertile valleys, and mountainous terrain, including areas like the Serra de la Safor. Cyclists can explore expansive orange groves and significant wetlands, such as the El Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park. The El Serpis Protected Landscape also provides immersive natural experiences along routes like the Serpis Greenway. Best touring cycling routes around Vilallonga / Villalonga The most popular touring cycling route is Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Route of the 8 Villages – Circular through the Vall de la Gallinera, a difficult 34.0 miles (54.7 km) path. This route connects multiple scenic villages, offering varied landscapes and challenging climbs. Local touring cyclists also love the Serpis River – Azud del Infierno or del Forn loop from L'Udol Reprimala, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through the Serpis River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Vilallonga / Villalonga is defined by coastal views, agricultural plains, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely rides through orange groves to demanding climbs. The routes in Vilallonga / Villalonga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 3900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilallonga / Villalonga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vilallonga?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Vilallonga, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. The komoot community has highly rated these routes, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Vilallonga offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. The region features coastal paths and greenways, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1616995/cycling-around-vilallonga-villalonga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Safor&lt;/a&gt;, which is mostly flat and well-paved, passing through orange groves and Mediterranean vegetation. This trail is ideal for leisurely excursions and includes picnic areas.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Vilallonga?
A: Touring cycling in Vilallonga offers a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll encounter Mediterranean coastlines, fertile valleys, expansive orange groves, and significant wetlands like the El Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park. The mountainous terrain of the Serra de la Safor also provides challenging climbs and stunning views.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952611441/dawn-s-way-and-coll-de-la-safor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952650716/the-route-of-the-8-villages-circular-through-the-vall-de-la-gallinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the 8 Villages – Circular through the Vall de la Gallinera&lt;/a&gt;, which connects multiple scenic villages with varied landscapes and challenging climbs.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Vilallonga are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952650716/the-route-of-the-8-villages-circular-through-the-vall-de-la-gallinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the 8 Villages&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38272782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serpis River – Azud del Infierno or del Forn loop from L'Udol Reprimala&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can visit along the routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore several natural attractions. The El Serpis Protected Landscape is a highlight, with routes like the Serpis Greenway winding through it. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4866511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural area of the "Font Salà"&lt;/a&gt;, known for its warm, sulfur-rich waters. For panoramic views, consider routes near peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de la Safor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Vilallonga?
A: The La Safor region, where Vilallonga is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Even winter can be suitable for cycling, especially for those looking to escape colder climates, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there routes that pass through the El Serpis Protected Landscape?
A: Yes, the El Serpis Protected Landscape is a significant feature of the region, and several routes, such as the Serpis Greenway, wind through it. These routes offer an immersive natural experience along the Serpis River valley, providing scenic views and a tranquil environment.

Q: Is the Vía Verde de la Safor suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Safor is a significant draw for touring cyclists. This former railway line is mostly flat and well-paved, connecting Gandia with Oliva. It's perfect for leisurely rides, passing through picturesque orange groves and Mediterranean vegetation, and includes picnic areas for breaks.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Vilallonga?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vilallonga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to challenging mountain passes, and the variety of routes catering to all ability levels. Many appreciate the immersive natural experiences through orange groves and protected wetlands.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the coast or pass through orange groves?
A: Yes, the region's diverse topography includes both Mediterranean coastlines and expansive orange groves. Many routes, particularly the easier ones and the Vía Verde de la Safor, will take you through these fragrant groves. While Vilallonga itself is inland, some routes extend towards the coast, offering glimpses of the sea.

Q: Are there any routes that connect multiple villages?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy exploring local culture and scenery, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952650716/the-route-of-the-8-villages-circular-through-the-vall-de-la-gallinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the 8 Villages – Circular through the Vall de la Gallinera&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices. This challenging route connects several scenic villages, offering a unique way to experience the region's charm and varied landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop?
faqs.answer: This route offers a diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter significant climbs, particularly on the Coll de la Safor, which features steep gradients and can have some poorer road surfaces. There are also sections of gravel, such as on the Serpis Greenway, and some parts may require very good fitness, potentially even pushing your bike on the steepest segments.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain (over 730 meters) and challenging climbs like the Coll de la Safor. It is best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The loop offers several notable sights. You'll pass a Ford crossing over the Serpis River, a unique Bridge of pipes, and enjoy Views of the Sorollosa Waterfall. The route also includes a Serpis Greenway (gravel section), the historic L'Orxa Station, and offers Views of Perputxent Castle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for the 'Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop' isn't detailed, the route often starts or passes through villages like Villalonga or L'Orxa. Look for public parking areas within these towns, which typically offer convenient access to the trailheads.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The La Safor region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the hottest summer months. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, especially on exposed climbs.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in this region, especially if kept on a leash. However, as parts of the route pass through the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes, it's always best to keep your dog under control to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route or the natural park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Dawn's Way and Coll de la Safor Loop or to enter the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What should I know about cycling within the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes?
faqs.answer: When cycling within the Paratge Natural Municipal Penyes Albes, please adhere to general park regulations. Stay on marked trails, do not disturb wildlife, and carry out all your trash. The park is a protected area, so minimizing your impact is crucial to preserving its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near small villages like L'Orxa. While options might be limited, you may find small shops or cafes in these settlements to refill water bottles or grab a snack. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and provisions, especially given the route's difficulty and length.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' classification, significant elevation, and sections of gravel on the Serpis Greenway, a robust touring bicycle or a gravel bike with appropriate gearing for climbing and wider tires for varied surfaces would be ideal. A road bike might struggle with some of the poorer road surfaces and gravel sections.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll cycle along parts of the Camino del Alba, l'Orxa - Àrees Recreatives, and the challenging [CIMA VA04] Coll de la Safor (l'Orxa). It also briefly touches the Via Verde del Serpis and other local paths like Camí de les Majones.
